title: No quay container image available for release 1.18.0
body:
Hi,

just noticed that there is no 1.18.0 container image available on quay. Is that a known issue?

comments.body:
F40: https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2025-d243e027e4
F41: https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2025-92d3942828

We bundle all our  updates in one single bodhi update via packit, the f41 update should hit stable soon and then the container build process should pick it up. The stable container builds are all based on the latest stable fedora versions.
Ah, my mistake, Fedora 42 is not yet released.
But I think you are right, F42 update seems to be missing. That said AFAICT f42 is frozen now for the beta so no updates will get into stable there regardless.

> Guessing, is https://github.com/containers/skopeo/runs/37159021770 related?

That seems to be because of https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/2519#issuecomment-2656306585 . I've asked for packit to not retry PR creation for already successful branches with such github comments.

-----
Also, RE: skopeo 1.18.0 on F42..

bodhi created at https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2025-4616154cff . skopeo was built already by packit to a sidetag but that update was waiting for podman, buildah and c/common builds.

AFAICT, what happened was after [podman 5.4.0 got built for f42 on Feb 11](https://koji.fedoraproject.org/koji/buildinfo?buildID=2657439), I got that into bodhi with `/packit koji-tag` for [skopeo](https://src.fedoraproject.org/rpms/skopeo/pull-request/64#comment-246042) and others. [That bodhi](https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2025-049ef6c585) passed all gating tests and soon landed in stable. And then [skopeo 1.18.0 got built for f42 on Feb 13](https://koji.fedoraproject.org/koji/buildinfo?buildID=2658840) to a new sidetag and that kept waiting for other builds to land in that sidetag

… and v1.18.0 is now visible on Quay.

Thanks for the help!
